title: VDD: parse_findings uses Uuid::new_v4 per finding and Finding.id as HashMap key — test determinism broken
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Testability Coverage. Location: src/vdd/mod.rs:777 (Uuid::new_v4 in parse_findings) and 1037-1056 (verdicts map keyed by finding.id). Violation: Every parse_findings call generates fresh UUIDs, so the verification agent receives a new finding_id on every iteration. There is no stable identity for a finding across iterations: if the adversary re-reports the same SQL-injection on iteration 2 and 3, they get different UUIDs, making FP-history-based deduplication by ID impossible (which is why the code uses string_similarity — see issue on Jaccard weakness). More critically, tests cannot assert specific finding IDs because they are randomised every run. Mandated Refactor: Compute finding.id as a deterministic hash of (severity, cwe, file, line_range, description_hash). This enables cross-iteration dedup by ID (replacing the weak Jaccard), makes tests assertable, and gives Chainlink a stable natural key.
